title: Almond Cakes
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- two cupfuls of rice flour
- one quarter cupful of almond oil
- one half cupful of chopped almonds
- one and one half cupfuls of powdered sugar
- two eggs

## Directions

1. Mix thoroughly two cupfuls of rice flour, one and one half cupfuls of powdered sugar, and half a cupful of blanched almonds, chopped very fine, with a quarter of a cupful of almond oil.
2. Moisten with two beaten eggs.
3. Use no water, and if too stiff, add more egg.
4. Roll about quarter of an inch thick, and cut in fanciful shapes.
5. Place half an almond in the center of each cake, and bake them for one hour in a moderate oven.

title: Amati Tamana (Sweet and Sour Cabbage)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- small hard cabbage
- goma oil
- salt
- sugar
- vinegar
- lemon or orange peel
- goma seeds

## Directions

1. Cut a small, hard cabbage very fine.
2. Place in frying pan one tablespoonful of goma oil, and when it is boiling hot throw in the shredded cabbage.
3. Sprinkle with salt, and fry, stirring constantly to keep it from burning.
4. Add one tablespoonful of sugar, the same quantity of vinegar, and a little piece of lemon or orange peel, shaved very fine.
5. Cover tight, and let all simmer for twenty minutes.
6. Finally add half a teaspoon of goma seeds.
7. Serve hot.

title: Apple Jelly, Japanese Style
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- one half pound of seaweed jelly
- three quarters pound of sugar
- two big, tart apples
- two tablespoonfuls of kudzu starch
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. After washing half a pound of seaweed jelly soak it in water for about three hours, then squeeze it dry and break in small pieces into a pan with one and a half quarts of water.
2. Boil over medium heat.
3. Add three quarters of a pound of sugar, stirring the while, and a pinch of salt.
4. Prepare two big, tart apples by peeling and removing the cores, then rub them on a nutmeg grater until they are reduced to powder.
5. Transfer jelly to another saucepan, strain it, and mix with powdered apple.
6. When thoroughly boiled, add two tablespoonfuls of kudzu starch, dissolved in cold water.
7. Stir well with wooden chopsticks or spoon.
8. Remove from fire, and pour into lacquered box or molds.
9. Two or three hours after it has hardened, cut in desired shapes and sizes.

title: Bak Toy Gun (Chinese Soup of White Vegetables)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoonfuls of syou
- one half pound of white mushrooms
- one dozen water chestnuts
- one quarter pound of bean sprouts
- one stalk of celery
- two small onions
- three eggs
- bit of garlic
- three pints of boiling, salted water

## Directions

1. Have ready three pints of boiling, salted water, with the two tablespoonfuls of syou in it
2. Add all vegetables (which have been previously washed in cold water, and cut in small pieces) except the mushrooms and celery
3. Boil for fifteen minutes, then add the mushrooms and celery
4. Whip up the three eggs in a little cold water, and drop slowly into the boiling soup, stirring the soup as it drops
5. Remove at once, and serve

title: Baked Bananas
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- bananas
- chopped dates
- almonds
- powdered rice or sugar

## Directions

1. Split the bananas on one side of the skin.
2. Place in a medium oven and bake for about ten minutes.
3. Take the fruit carefully from the skin, taking pains not to break the skin.
4. Whip smooth with chopped dates and almonds (about a teaspoonful of dates and nuts to each banana).
5. Return mixture to shell, and serve with powdered rice or sugar.
6. Serve cold or hot.

title: Beef Kidney with Chinese Mushrooms
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- One beef kidney
- one quarter pound of dried mushrooms
- one small onion
- one and one half teaspoonfuls of salt
- dash of cayenne pepper
- two tablespoonfuls of chopped fat pork
- three tablespoonfuls of syou

## Directions

1. Soak the kidney for half an hour in cold water with a handful of salt; then cut in small pieces and fry in pork fat.
2. Wash a quarter of a pound of dried mushrooms in lukewarm water, pull off the stalks, and soak for ten minutes.
3. Add this to the kidney, with one small chopped onion, one and one quarter teaspoonfuls of salt, and three tablespoonfuls of syou.
4. Cover tight, and boil very slowly for one hour, adding boiling water if it dries.
5. Serve with rice.

title: Boiled and Deviled Cucumbers
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- salt
- water
- one tablespoonful of olive oil
- one teaspoonful of vinegar
- a dash of cayenne
- yolks of two hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Peel the cucumbers, and place in salted boiling water.
2. Boil slowly until the cucumbers become transparent.
3. Drain off all water and serve with a sauce made of one tablespoonful of olive oil and one teaspoonful of vinegar, beaten well with a dash of cayenne and the yolks of two hard-boiled eggs.

title: Boo Loo Gai (Pineapple Chicken)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- One young chicken
- one tablespoonful of sweet lard
- one tablespoonful of syou
- one can of preserved pineapple
- one teaspoonful of Quong Sang Chong

## Directions

1. Wash and singe a young, fresh chicken and cut off all the flesh.
2. Slice it, put a tablespoonful of sweet lard in the pan, and fry.
3. Do not let it burn.
4. Add the chicken, and fry brown.
5. Add a tablespoonful of syou and a can of preserved pineapple, and cook slowly for fifteen minutes.
6. Thicken the pineapple juice with a teaspoonful of Quong Sang Chong, and serve hot with rice.

title: Char Qua (Chinese Artichokes)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- six char quars
- one teaspoonful of olive oil
- one tablespoonful of Quong Sang Chong
- two tablespoonfuls of syou
- two tablespoonfuls of chopped parsley
- yolks of two hard-boiled eggs
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. First wash thoroughly in cold water, then turn them into boiling salted water and boil about twenty minutes.
2. Meanwhile prepare the following sauce: Mix one teaspoonful of olive oil with one tablespoonful of Quong Sang Chong (water chestnut flour) until very smooth, then pour enough boiling water over it to make a thick cream.
3. Add one teaspoonful of syou, and let it just come to a boil, stirring in the chopped parsley gently.
4. Arrange the artichokes on a small platter with green lining, or place a green leaf under each artichoke.
5. Fill the center of each with the yolks of hard-boiled egg, whipped smooth with a little olive oil and salt.
6. Lastly, pour the sauce over, and serve.
7. This should look like a water lily.

title: Chestnut Kintons (Cream Candy)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of chestnuts
- one pint of sweet potatoes
- one pound of sugar
- three quarters pint of mirin sauce
- one tablespoonful of vinegar

## Directions

1. Blanch the chestnuts and place them in lukewarm water.
2. Boil, changing water several times, until you can pierce the nuts with a needle.
3. Strain off water, then add one pound of sugar, three quarters of a pint of mirin sauce, and boil slowly.
4. Have ready sweet potatoes which have been peeled and boiled for twenty minutes.
5. Add to them the vinegar, and strain and press through sieve.
6. Gradually mix with the chestnuts, and slowly boil, stirring constantly, until dry; then mold into desired shapes.
7. Roll in powdered sugar.

title: Chicken Chow Main
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- one teaspoonful of salt
- one quart of peanut oil
- one half pound of noodles
- four ounces of pork
- two pounds of chopped chicken
- one stalk of celery
- one onion
- one half pound of fresh mushrooms
- one half pound of breast of chicken, shredded
- three hard-boiled eggs
- two tablespoonfuls of syou

## Directions

1. Have the peanut oil boiling hot and toss in the noodles.
2. Fry until they are crisp, then take them from the oil and drain, while preparing the following:
3. Take four ounces of fine chopped pork and half a pound of chicken meat, also chopped.
4. Add two tablespoonfuls of syou and one teaspoonful of salt.
5. Cook all for ten minutes.
6. Lay the noodles on a platter, forming a layer at the bottom of the dish, and place the vegetables and gravy on top of the noodles.
7. Add a layer of the shredded chicken breast, lastly the yolks of hard-boiled eggs, crumbled, as a garnish.
8. Serve very hot.

title: Chicken Liver Paste
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken livers
- fresh or dried mushrooms
- onion juice
- lemon juice
- salted crackers

## Directions

1. Fry chicken livers over slow fire, or boil if preferred.
2. Drain from water or fat when cooked, and crush through sieve.
3. Fry a cupful of fresh or dried mushrooms.
4. Crush these also through sieve, and add it to the livers.
5. Add the juice of an onion and some lemon juice.
6. Serve on salted crackers.

title: Chicken with Mushrooms
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- One tablespoonful of sweet lard
- two pounds of chicken
- one onion
- one pound of fresh mushrooms
- one tablespoonful of syou
- two teaspoonfuls of salt

## Directions

1. Put the lard in the pan, and have it boiling hot.
2. Cut up a young chicken, and remove all the bones; then lay it in the fat, and cook to a golden brown.
3. Mince the onion and add.
4. Wash and cut up the mushrooms, and add with a tablespoonful of syou.
5. Cook all slowly for fifteen minutes.

title: Chinese Fried Peas
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- one teaspoonful of peanut oil
- one pint of yellow whole peas

## Directions

1. Put a teaspoonful of peanut oil in the frying pan, taking care to have one with a long handle, as the peas may pop.
2. Toss a pint of yellow whole peas in the pan, and shake the pan while the peas roll about and turn a light brown.

title: Chinese Fried Pork with Onions
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of pork
- two large onions
- two tablespoonfuls of syou
- one teaspoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Cut about a pound of fresh pork (not too fat) in tubes, and fry a light brown.
2. Cut up two large onions and fry with the pork until all is brown, then add the salt and two tablespoonfuls of syou, and fry very hot.
3. This is very tasty, and good served with boiled rice.

title: Chinese Pickled Yellow Turnips
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- turnip
- two cupfuls of vinegar
- a dash of cayenne
- one quarter of a clove of garlic
- two teaspoonfuls of salt
- one teaspoonful of ginger

## Directions

1. Cut the turnip in thin slices, then cut each slice in narrow strips until it looks like strands.
2. Place in bowl and over it pour two cupfuls of vinegar, a dash of cayenne, a quarter of a clove of garlic, two teaspoonfuls of salt, and one teaspoonful of ginger, and let stand twenty-four hours before serving.

title: Chinese Scrambled Eggs
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- one half teaspoon of sweet lard
- one onion
- five eggs
- one teaspoonful of salt
- dash of cayenne pepper or black pepper

## Directions

1. Put half a tablespoonful of lard in frying pan and when it is very hot toss in one onion, chopped fine, and fry to a golden brown.
2. Break five eggs in a bowl, beat well, and turn into the pan; then keep stirring until it is done.
3. It should all be a light golden brown.
4. This is very good with rice.

title: Chop Suey
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- One and one half pounds of pork
- One and one half pounds of veal
- Two onions
- One dozen water chestnuts
- One half can of bamboo shoots
- Two pounds of bean sprouts
- Three tablespoonfuls of syou
- One half tablespoonful of salt
- One half bunch of celery

## Directions

1. Cut half a pound of pork in small pieces, and fry for three minutes.
2. Cut up the veal and add it, frying for five minutes.
3. Chop up two onions and half a bunch of celery into small pieces, slice thin a dozen water chestnuts and half a can of bamboo shoots, and turn into the pan with three tablespoonfuls of syou and half a tablespoonful of salt.
4. Cook for ten minutes.
5. Add the bean sprouts, and cook all together for five minutes.
6. Serve with rice.

title: Cold Pickled Fish
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of fish
- one pint of vinegar
- one pint of water
- four red peppers
- one tablespoonful of salt
- one teaspoonful of sugar

## Directions

1. Wash the fish, then wipe dry and lay it in an earthen dish
2. Mix together one pint of vinegar and one pint of water, and when boiling, pour it over the fish
3. Cut up four red peppers, taking out the seeds
4. Sprinkle the salt and sugar over the fish, and then lay in the peppers
5. Cover tight, and put away for two hours
6. Drain off all vinegar and water

title: Curry Shrimps
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- One onion
- One tablespoonful of olive oil
- One and one half teaspoonfuls of curry powder
- One tablespoonful of syou
- One cupful of boiling water
- One tablespoonful of rice flour
- One teaspoonful of salt
- One and one half pounds of boiled shrimps
- A dash of cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Fry to a light brown in one tablespoonful of pure olive oil, one onion, chopped fine.
2. Mix one and one half teaspoonfuls of curry powder with a little cold water to make a smooth paste, and stir it in.
3. Add a tablespoonful of syou, a cupful of boiling water, a teaspoonful of salt, a dash of cayenne pepper, and dust with rice powder, stirring until it thickens.
4. Have ready a pound of cooked shrimps, flaked, or white fish may be used instead.
5. Simmer for five minutes, and serve with rice.

title: Dai-Kan (Orange Jelly Candy)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- one half pound of Kanton (seaweed jelly)
- three quarters pound of fine white sugar
- three tablespoonfuls of kudzu starch
- two oranges

## Directions

1. Wash half a pound of dry seaweed jelly, and soak it for two and a half hours in cold water.
2. Then squeeze out all the water and break in small pieces.
3. Boil in about a quart of water, until well melted, then mix three quarters of a pound of sugar with this, stirring with chopsticks or wooden spoon.
4. Strain into another pan, and boil over medium fire.
5. Take three tablespoonfuls of kudzu starch and dissolve in a little cold water.
6. Then, drop by drop, gradually drop this into the jelly, while stirring with wooden spoon.
7. When it becomes transparent, squeeze in the juice of two oranges and immediately remove from fire.
8. Pour into a wooden box, and set in cool place for two hours to harden.

title: Dried Shrimp Stew
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- Two cupfuls of dried shrimp
- One tablespoonful of rice flour
- One teaspoonful of salt
- A dash of cayenne pepper
- One and one half tablespoonfuls of syou

## Directions

1. Soak the shrimps in cold water for half an hour, drain, and cover with cold water, add the cayenne pepper and syou, and boil slowly about one hour.
2. Mix the rice flour with cold water to a smooth paste, and stir until it thickens.
3. Serve with rice.

title: Duck Chop Suey
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- Three or three and one half pound duck
- One tablespoonful of duck fat
- One and one quarter tablespoonfuls of syou
- Dash of cayenne pepper
- Two teaspoonfuls of salt
- One cup of dried mushrooms
- One bunch of celery
- One half cup of small white onions
- One dozen lotus seeds
- One can of bamboo shoots
- Two pounds of bean sprouts

## Directions

1. Carefully wash the duck and remove the bones, then wipe dry and pound the meat until tender.
2. Chop up about a tablespoonful of duck fat, and fry.
3. Remove all lumps of fat, leaving only the clear oil, and put in the duck meat, cut in small pieces.
4. Fry to a golden brown.
5. Add one and one half tablespoonfuls of syou, a dash of cayenne pepper, and half a tablespoonful of salt.
6. Cover, and let simmer for twenty minutes while preparing the following: Wash and soak for ten minutes one cup of dried mushrooms, pulling off all stalks and cutting small; cut up a bunch of celery small, and add a cupful of small white onions.
7. Slice a dozen lotus seeds very thin, and half a can of bamboo shoots.
8. Put all in with the duck and fry ten minutes; then add two pounds of bean sprouts and cook five minutes longer.
9. Serve with rice.

title: Duck With Herbs
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- Three to four pound duck
- One quarter pound of pork fat
- One onion
- Two stalks of celery
- One lemon peeling
- One quarter clove of garlic
- Two tablespoonfuls of syou
- One tablespoonful of mixed spices
- One cup of stock
- One dozen mushrooms
- One teaspoonful of salt
- One tablespoonful of parsley

## Directions

1. Drain and singe the duck.
2. Wipe, then thoroughly dry, inside and out, with a clean, damp cloth.
3. Line a pan with small pieces of pork (fat).
4. Sprinkle the bottom with minced onion, celery, grated lemon peel, and the garlic, grated fine.
5. Lay the duck in and cover with a tablespoonful of mixed spices, two tablespoonfuls of syou, and a cup of rich stock.
6. Set over the fire and simmer for two hours, basting often.
7. Wash the mushrooms and turn them in, and cook all together for ten minutes.
8. Take up, and thicken the gravy with cornstarch or rice flour.
9. Lastly, toss in the chopped parsley.
10. Cut up the duck in small pieces (not too small), arrange on dish, pour the gravy with the herbs around the duck, and serve hot with rice.

title: Egg Plant
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- one large egg plant
- two cups of vinegar
- one dessert-spoonful of mixed spices
- one cup of sugar
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Cut one large egg plant in slices (not too thin).
2. Soak for two hours in cold water with a handful of salt.
3. Then drain this off, and pour over the egg plant a little vinegar mixed with water, and let it lie in this for about half an hour.
4. Then drain thoroughly and pour over it two cupfuls of hot vinegar with dessert-spoonful of mixed spices and one cup of sugar.
5. Cover tight and put away for at least twenty-four hours.

title: Endive
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- salt
- a tiny piece of washing soda
- endive
- one tablespoonful of olive oil
- one tablespoonful of vinegar
- a dash of cayenne
- one half teaspoonful of sugar
- one teaspoonful of salt
- one clove of garlic

## Directions

1. Have water boiling hot and salted.
2. Now put in a tiny piece of washing soda.
3. Wash the endive, throw it into boiling water, and boil slowly for ten minutes.
4. While boiling, take one tablespoonful of olive oil and heat it.
5. Add one tablespoonful of vinegar, a dash of cayenne, half a teaspoonful of sugar, a teaspoonful of salt, and one clove of garlic, chopped very fine.
6. Let this all simmer for five minutes, then drain off the water, and pour out the endive, and serve.

title: Extra White Chop Suey
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- Breast of one and one half pound chicken
- One tablespoonful of chicken fat
- One pound of fresh mushrooms
- One half bunch of celery
- One dozen water chestnuts
- Two white onions
- One half can of bamboo shoots
- Two pounds of bean sprouts
- One and one half teaspoonfuls of salt
- Two tablespoonfuls of syou
- Dash of cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Cut in small pieces the breast of a young chicken of about one and one half pounds.
2. Put a tablespoonful of chicken fat in a deep frying pan, and heat very hot, then put in the chicken and fry brown, stirring to keep from burning.
3. Have ready one pound of fresh white mushrooms, cut small; half a bunch of celery, chopped; a dozen water chestnuts, peeled and cut in slices; two white onions, and half a can of bamboo shoots, all sliced.
4. Add all these to the chicken, and cook for ten minutes.
5. Add two pounds of bean sprouts, and cook for another five minutes with two tablespoonfuls of syou and a dash of cayenne pepper.
6. Simmer for five minutes longer, and serve with rice.

title: Foo Tay Dan (Chinese Ham and Eggs)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- one tablespoonful of ham fat
- one half cup of minced ham
- one half dozen water chestnuts
- one half of a small onion

## Directions

1. Heat one tablespoonful of ham fat very hot.
2. Chop up half a cupful of ham meat; slice thin half a dozen water chestnuts and half a small onion.
3. Fry all together for five minutes; then beat six eggs, turn in pan, and scramble.

title: Foo Yung Dan (Chinese Omelette with Herbs)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- one half a minced onion
- four sticks of celery
- four ounces of pork
- one dessert-spoonful of syou
- one half teaspoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Beat four eggs well; have ready half a minced onion and four sticks of celery, chopped very fine.
2. Put in the frying pan four ounces of pork, chopped fine, and fry until brown.
3. Now add the herbs, with a dessert-spoonful of syou, and finally the beaten eggs.
4. Let the whole cook for five minutes, without touching, but be careful to keep it from burning.
5. Fold one half over the other, and slip on the platter.
6. Serve at once, with rice.

title: Fried Bamboo Shoots
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- one can of bamboo shoots
- boiling peanut oil

## Directions

1. Take one can of bamboo shoots and drain off all water.
2. Wipe the bamboo shoots dry, and slice in long thin strips.
3. Have ready boiling peanut oil, and toss the shoots into that.
4. Cook until crisp.
5. Delicious. Must be eaten hot.

title: Fried Bean Sprouts
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter of a pound of fresh pork fat
- one pound of bean sprouts
- three tablespoonfuls of syou
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Into a hot iron pan put a quarter of a pound of fresh pork fat, and fry brown.
2. Drain all water from a pound of bean sprouts, put into the hot fat, and fry uncovered for five minutes, stirring to keep from burning.
3. Add to the three tablespoonfuls of syou, salt, and pepper.
4. Cover tight, and let simmer for fifteen minutes.

title: Fried Beef with Mushrooms
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of beef
- one pound of mushrooms
- one small onion
- six lotus seeds
- two tablespoonfuls of syou
- one teaspoonful of salt
- one half pound of suet

## Directions

1. Use the upper part of the round, or rump steak, cut in small pieces (strips about two inches long).
2. Chop up the suet and fry out all the fat, drain, and fry the beef in it good and brown.
3. Add the onion and slice the lotus seeds.
4. Cut up the mushrooms and turn in.
5. Fry for about two minutes, then add two tablespoonfuls of syou and salt.
6. Cover up tight, and let it simmer for fifteen minutes.
7. Serve with rice.

title: Fried Cabbage
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- two tablespoonfuls of hot fat
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Wash and dry the cabbage, then cut in long, slender strips, and throw into a deep frying pan containing two tablespoonfuls of hot fat.
2. Sprinkle with salt and pepper to taste and fry, stirring for five minutes, shaking the pan to keep cabbage from burning.
3. This is very good with rice, and is used by the poor Chinese as a substitute for fish.

title: Fried Cabbage
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- hard cabbage
- olive oil
- pepper
- salt
- syou sauce

## Directions

1. Wash and shred a hard cabbage.
2. Put olive oil in deep iron pot, and add the cabbage, pressing down until it is absorbed into the oil and moisture.
3. Add pepper, salt, and syou sauce, and let simmer slowly for half an hour.
4. Remove lid about ten minutes before serving, to allow cabbage to brown.

title: Fried Chicken (Chinese Style)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- Two and one half pounds of chicken
- Two tablespoonfuls of sweet lard
- One tablespoonful of syou
- One half onion
- One teaspoonful of cornstarch or Quong Sang Chong
- One teaspoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Clean a young spring chicken, and cut it into small pieces.
2. Fry to a golden brown in hot, sweet lard.
3. Serve with a brown gravy prepared as follows: Boil the giblets until tender, then chop very fine and return to the liquor in which they were boiled.
4. Add a tablespoonful of syou and half an onion chopped fine; boil for ten minutes.
5. Thicken with a teaspoonful of cornstarch or rice flour, and pour over the chicken.
6. Serve hot, with rice.

title: Fried Noodles
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- about two quarts of boiling peanut oil
- noodles

## Directions

1. Have about two quarts of boiling peanut oil.
2. Throw in the noodles and fry a golden brown.
3. Remove from oil, and drain off all fat by laying noodles on bit of paper.

title: Fried Rice With Chicken and Mushrooms
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of rice
- One pound of cooked flesh of chicken
- One pound of fresh white mushrooms
- Two stalks of celery
- One onion
- Six water chestnuts
- One tablespoonful of pork fat
- Two tablespoonfuls of syou
- Dash of cayenne pepper
- One teaspoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Boil one pound of rice for twenty minutes.
2. While this is boiling, take one pound of cooked chicken and cut it in small pieces.
3. Put a tablespoonful of pork fat in the pan, and when it is very hot turn in the chicken and fry for a few minutes.
4. Cut up two stalks of celery, one onion, and six water chestnuts, and add with the syou cayenne, and salt.
5. Fry all for ten minutes, then place the rice in a platter and pour the above over it.
6. Cover tight, and let it soak through the rice thoroughly.
7. Garnish with chopped parsley, and serve.

title: Fried Rice with Eggs and Herbs
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- one half pound of rice
- one quarter pound of pork, very fat
- three water chestnuts
- one stalk of celery
- one onion
- one teaspoonful of chopped parsley
- one teaspoonful of salt
- pinch of pepper

## Directions

1. First wash the rice and boil for half an hour.
2. Cut up the pork very small, and fry brown.
3. Add one onion, chopped fine; the three water chestnuts, sliced thin, and one stalk of celery, cut small.
4. Salt and pepper, and fry all for ten minutes, taking care not to brown.
5. Now turn in the rice, and fry five minutes.
6. Meanwhile beat up six eggs, pour them over the mixture, stirring and shaking the pan constantly for five minutes, and serve hot.

title: Fried Rice with Herbs
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- two cupfuls of boiled rice
- one and one half teaspoonfuls of pork fat
- three stalks of celery
- one large onion
- five water chestnuts
- one teaspoonful of salt
- dash cayenne pepper
- two tablespoonfuls of syou

## Directions

1. Fry one large onion a light brown in one and one half tablespoonfuls of pork fat.
2. Chop up three stalks of celery very fine, and add five water chestnuts, sliced thin.
3. Fry all a light brown, then take two cups of rice that has boiled for twenty-five minutes, or use cold rice if you have any on hand.
4. Mix all together with salt and cayenne and syou, and fry for ten minutes, shaking and stirring constantly.
5. Serve hot, garnished with any meat or fish, or alone.

title: Fried Rice with Tomato Sauce
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- one teaspoonful of chopped parsley
- two cupfuls of boiled rice
- four tomatoes, or half a can
- one onion
- one tablespoonful of fat pork
- dash of cayenne

## Directions

1. Cut up the pork and fry brown.
2. Put in the onion, then the tomatoes and salt and cayenne.
3. Fry for five minutes.
4. Turn the rice, which has previously been cooked, into the pan and fry all together for five minutes.
5. Place on hot platter, and garnish with minced parsley and eggs, threaded.
6. Serve very hot.

title: Fried Squab
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- very small squabs
- peanut oil

## Directions

1. Take very small squabs, clean them, and rub dry.
2. Separate the legs and wings, and cut the body into small pieces.
3. Toss into boiling peanut oil, and cook until crisp.
4. Remove, drain off oil, and serve on fried Tofu.

title: Gai Grun Yung Waa (Bird's-nest Soup)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- one half pound of bird's nest
- one pint of chicken stock
- one quarter pound of cooked breast of chicken
- one boiled egg
- one quarter pound of minced ham
- one teaspoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Boil the bird's nest an hour, then drain and put into cold water
2. Pound the cooked chicken meat well so as not to be in large or hard pieces
3. Add a cupful of the cold stock to the chicken meat
4. Take the bird's nest from the cold water and well drain it
5. Add the bird's nest to the soup stock
6. Boil for half an hour
7. Add the chicken meat, and also the egg, the latter having previously been finely crumbled
8. Take the soup off the fire as it begins to boil again after the last addition
9. Before serving, sprinkle the minced ham on top

title: Gai Yuk Chee Yuk
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- One half pound of breast of chicken
- One half pound of lean pork
- Three tablespoonfuls of sweet lard
- One half pound of mushrooms
- One half bunch of celery
- One dozen lotus seeds
- One half can of bamboo shoots
- Two pounds of bean sprouts
- One and one half teaspoonfuls of syou
- One half teaspoonful of salt
- Dash of cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Take half a pound of chicken cut from the breast and half a pound of lean pork, and cut both into small pieces.
2. Heat three tablespoonfuls of sweet lard; when it is well melted, put the above meat in the fat and fry until brown, stirring to keep it from burning.
3. Have ready one half pound of fresh or dried mushrooms which have been washed in lukewarm water (if dried mushrooms are used, soak them for ten minutes and pull off the stalks), half a bunch of celery chopped small, a dozen lotus seeds or water chestnuts peeled and cut into thin slices.
4. Cut up one onion, also half a can of bamboo shoots and two pounds of bean sprouts.
5. Wash all well and drain in colander.
6. Put all these, except the bean sprouts, with the meat, and cook for ten minutes; now add the bean sprouts, one and one half tablespoonfuls of syou, a dash of cayenne pepper, and salt, and cook for five minutes.
7. Serve with rice.

title: Gar Lu Chop Suey
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- One half pound of pork
- One half pound of beef
- One quarter clove of garlic
- One onion
- One half bunch of celery
- One dozen lotus seeds
- One half can of bamboo shoots
- One and one half pounds of bean sprouts
- Three tablespoonfuls of syou
- One teaspoonful of salt
- Dash cayenne pepper
- Chinese dried mushrooms

## Directions

1. Cut half a pound of pork (not too fat) into small pieces, and fry to a nice golden brown.
2. Chop into small pieces half a bunch of celery, and wash and soak Chinese dried mushrooms, discarding the stalks.
3. Chop up one onion, and a very small piece of garlic, chopped very fine.
4. Season with three tablespoonfuls of syou, salt, and add a dash of cayenne.
5. Fry for ten minutes, then add half a can of bamboo shoots and fry for five minutes longer.
6. Lastly, add one and one half pounds of bean sprouts, and cook all together for ten minutes.
7. Serve with rice.

title: Giblet Paste
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- giblets
- onion juice
- lemon juice
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Boil giblets until they are in shreds.
2. Crush through sieve, then add the juice of an onion and lemon juice.
3. Whip lightly with the hard-boiled yolk of eggs.
4. Serve on crackers, with crumbled yolks of eggs sprinkled on top.

title: Gum Lu (Golden Cakes)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- one and one half cupfuls of rice flour
- one cupful of honey
- one quarter cupful of mixed nuts, chopped
- three teaspoonfuls of clarified goose fat
- yolks of two eggs
- pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Take one and one half cupfuls of rice flour and a pinch of salt and into this work three teaspoonfuls of clarified goose fat.
2. Then chop very fine about quarter of a cup of minced nuts.
3. Beat the yolks of two eggs, and mix all together.
4. Now pour in one cup of raw, dark honey.
5. If too moist, add more flour.
6. Stir thoroughly for fifteen or twenty minutes, and pour into small cake pans, well oiled, and bake slowly for two hours.

title: Hato Shiro (Stewed Pigeon)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- Two good-sized pigeons
- one cupful of syou sauce
- one half cupful of mirin sauce or sherry
- one tablespoonful of sugar
- one small onion
- two tablespoonfuls of kudzu starch
- one quarter pound of fat fresh pork
- one dozen water chestnuts
- one quarter pound of bamboo shoots
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Select good, plump pigeons, and singe and clean thoroughly, then dry.
2. Rub well with salt.
3. Take the fat pork and fry out all the fat, and when boiling hot put in the pigeons and brown all over, rolling them on all sides.
4. Have ready the water chestnuts, washed and cut in thin slices, also the bamboo shoots cut in thin strips.
5. Chop up the onion very fine, and turn into the saucepan with the pigeons, adding the syou sauce, pepper, salt, and sugar.
6. Cover very tight, and simmer over a slow fire for one hour.
7. Then add the mirin sauce, or sherry.
8. Mix two tablespoonfuls of kudzu starch in cold water, and stir into the juice until it thickens.
9. Serve with rice.

title: Hop Ho Gai Din (Fried Chicken with Almonds or Walnuts)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- Two and one half pound chicken
- two tablespoonfuls of olive oil
- one cupful of Chinese almonds
- two stalks of celery
- one teaspoonful of onion juice
- two tablespoonfuls of syou
- one quarter pound of white mushrooms
- one cupful of almonds or walnuts, blanched and chopped
- Quong Sang Chong

## Directions

1. Use only the breast of a young chicken, and cut it in small tubes.
2. Fry to a golden brown in two tablespoonfuls of olive oil.
3. Take a cupful of almonds or walnuts, blanched and chopped, and half a pound of small white mushrooms cut up small; to this add a teaspoonful of onion juice and two tablespoonfuls of syou.
4. Turn all in with the chicken and simmer half an hour.
5. Thicken with Quong Sang Chong, and garnish with a border of nuts.

title: Japanese Fish Balls
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled fish, cold
- rice
- flour
- goma-seed oil

## Directions

1. Take any boiled fish, cold, and mix with rice, boiled to a paste.
2. Roll in flour to balls the size of large marbles.
3. Toss into boiling goma-seed oil, and fry a golden brown.

title: Karefish Nigore (Sole Jelly)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- one medium-sized sole
- one cupful mirin sauce
- two carrots
- one quarter pound dried mushrooms
- one half pound Kanton (dried seaweed jelly)
- salt
- water
- sugar
- syou sauce

## Directions

1. First prepare the sole by cutting away the head and thoroughly cleaning and scaling.
2. Cut in fillets or slices, removing the small bones.
3. Take one cupful of mirin sauce and a pinch of salt.
4. Place in pot, and boil only a few minutes.
5. Chop into small pieces two carrots and about a quarter pound of dried mushrooms (first washed and soaked).
6. Boil in a small quantity of water, and when tender, strain.
7. Add the mirin sauce, previously boiled for fifteen minutes.
8. Wash half a pound of dry seaweed jelly, and soak in water for two hours.
9. Take out, and squeeze dry.
10. Break in small pieces, and throw in pan with a quart of water.
11. Boil and dissolve.
12. Strain into another saucepan.
13. Add half a cupful of sugar and a tablespoonful of syou sauce.
14. With this mix the fish and vegetables, and stir thoroughly.
15. Transfer to lacquered or smooth wooden box, and let it set.
16. When it is firm, cut in desired small shapes.

title: Kinoko-Tamago-Yaki (Shrimp Omelette)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- Four eggs
- one half cupful of shrimp meat
- one half cupful of syou sauce
- goma-seed oil

## Directions

1. Beat up the eggs with half a cupful of syou.
2. Have ready about four or five small frying pans.
3. Wipe pans when hot with paper soaked in goma-seed oil.
4. Pour into each pan an equal quantity of the egg, and quickly divide and scatter in the shrimp meat, which has been finely chopped.
5. Fry all slowly for four minutes.

title: Kuwai-Kinton (Water Chestnut Cream)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- Fifteen large water chestnuts
- three quarters pound of sweet potatoes
- three tablespoonfuls of vinegar
- three quarters pound of sugar
- one half pint of mirin sauce
- salt

## Directions

1. Peel the skin from fifteen large water chestnuts and slice them very thin
2. Boil in lukewarm water for about fifteen minutes
3. Peel the sweet potatoes, cut in slices, and dip in ice-cold water three or four times, changing the water with each dipping
4. Boil about one quart in an enameled saucepan, add the vinegar, then throw in the sweet potatoes
5. Cover, and boil for about twenty minutes
6. If tender, drain off all the water and add the sugar, with five tablespoonfuls of warm water and also the mirin sauce
7. Add a pinch of salt
8. Place over the fire and add the above prepared water chestnuts
9. When the water chestnuts have thoroughly absorbed the juice, add the strained sweet potatoes, stir with wooden spoon, taking care not to burn it, and boil until it is firm
10. Serve hot or cold

title: Lai Yut (Beautiful Moon Tarts)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- two cupfuls of rice flour
- one heaping tablespoonful of clarified goose fat
- two eggs
- ice-cold water
- two cups of lychee nuts, stoned and mashed to a pulp
- one and one half cupfuls of sugar
- quarter of a cupful of crystallized limes
- one teaspoonful of mixed spices
- candied orange peel or fruit coloring

## Directions

1. Work a heaping tablespoonful of clarified goose fat into two cups of rice flour.
2. Add ice-cold water slowly to make a stiff paste.
3. Roll out, brush with the whipped whites of eggs, and fold over several times, each time brushing with egg.
4. Roll about quarter of an inch thick and cut into rounds about the size of a small saucer.
5. Put in the filling, and place another round of paste on top.
6. Press together at the edge, forming a rounded edge, and brush all again with white of egg.
7. Bake for fifty minutes in a moderate oven, then remove the outer flake, which will leave the tart snow white.
8. Decorate with a yellow moon in the center, cut from candied orange peel or painted with fruit coloring.

title: Lobster Chow Main
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of lobster meat
- two eggs
- one half pound of noodles
- three stalks of celery
- one can of bamboo shoots
- one onion
- one quarter pound of mushrooms
- one tablespoonful of sweet lard
- one quart of peanut oil
- three water chestnuts

## Directions

1. First boil the peanut oil and into it throw the noodles, cooking until crisp.
2. Remove and drain, while preparing following:
3. Put teaspoon of sweet lard into frying pan, and when hot turn in half the lobster meat, which has previously been cooked.
4. Cook for a few minutes, then add the onion, mushrooms, and water chestnuts.
5. The bamboo shoots are added last, and all fried for ten minutes.
6. Take a hot platter, and place the crisp noodles as a layer at the bottom of the dish, and spread above ingredients on top.
7. Then take the other half of the lobster meat, and place a layer on top.
8. Garnish with shredded yolks of hard-boiled eggs.

title: Lobster Omelette
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- one teaspoonful of peanut oil
- two eggs
- one tablespoonful of cold water
- one tablespoonful of chopped lobster meat
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Use small frying pan and put into it a teaspoonful of peanut oil
2. Heat it
3. Beat two eggs with a tablespoonful of cold water
4. Pour half in the pan
5. Have ready the cooked lobster, broken into small pieces
6. Quickly pour in the other half of beaten eggs, and cook slowly for five minutes
7. Slip off pan without breaking, and make two or three more omelettes in exactly the same way, or have several small frying pans and cook all at once
8. Serve one omelette on top of another in a hot water-heated platter

title: Lychee Chicken
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- Two pound chicken
- one teaspoonful of salt
- one quart of lychee nuts
- two tablespoonfuls of syou
- one half small onion
- two tablespoonfuls of sweet lard

## Directions

1. Cut into small pieces a fresh young chicken of about two pounds and rub well with salt.
2. Fry two tablespoonfuls of sweet lard a very light brown.
3. Add two tablespoonfuls of syou, and cover tight.
4. Simmer for half an hour.
5. Have ready a quart of lychee nuts, peeled and stoned.
6. Add these to the chicken, with the minced onion, and cook slowly for twenty-six minutes.
7. Serve with rice.

title: Meat Chow Main
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter pound noodles
- one quart peanut oil
- one half dozen water chestnuts
- one half pound of pork
- one half pound of veal
- two tablespoonfuls of syou
- one half bunch of celery
- one onion
- one half pound of dried mushrooms
- two eggs
- one quarter pound of ham
- one half tablespoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Into a quart of peanut oil put a quarter of a pound of noodles, and cook until crisp; then remove and drain.
2. Meanwhile take one pound of pork, cut it in small pieces, and fry a golden brown.
3. Cut up half a pound of veal, and fry with the pork for five minutes.
4. Add two tablespoonfuls of syou and half a tablespoonful of salt to this, and let it simmer slowly, while preparing the following:
5. Wash, and soak for ten minutes, half a pound of Chinese dried mushrooms, pulling off the stalks; half a bunch of celery, cut small; also one onion, chopped fine, and half a dozen water chestnuts, sliced fine.
6. Turn these in with the meat and cook all together for five minutes.
7. Place the noodles on a hot platter as a bottom layer, then the meat and vegetables.
8. Garnish the top with threaded ham and the crumbled yolks of two hard-boiled eggs.

title: Mo Ku Gai T'ong (Spring Chicken Soup with Mushrooms)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- two and one half pounds of chicken
- one quarter pound of lean pork
- one quarter pound of dried mushrooms
- one dozen lotus seeds
- three stalks of celery
- one teaspoonful of salt
- one tablespoonful of syou
- one onion
- three pints of cold water

## Directions

1. Clean a fresh young chicken of about two and one half pounds
2. Cut off all the flesh; put the bones, with liver and gizzard, into three pints of cold water, and boil for two hours, adding water if it boils away
3. While the stock is boiling, cut up the chicken meat into small pieces
4. Cut up very fine one quarter pound of lean pork
5. Wash and soak in lukewarm water one quarter pound of dried mushrooms
6. Pull off all the stalks
7. Peel and cut a dozen lotus seeds or water chestnuts
8. Chop three stalks of celery very fine, and also one onion
9. Pour off the liquor from the bones, and strain
10. Cut up liver and gizzard fine and return to stock
11. Add all the ingredients, with one teaspoonful of salt and one tablespoonful of syou
12. Boil all together for one hour

title: Mushi Ahiru (Baked Duck)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- duck
- salt
- kudzu starch
- eggs
- oil
- giblets
- one cupful of water
- one cupful of syou sauce
- pepper
- one teaspoonful of sugar
- orange or lemon peel

## Directions

1. Clean and singe the duck and wash in lukewarm water, then wipe dry with a clean cloth.
2. Rub well with salt.
3. Cut in pieces, and dip each piece of the duck in a batter made of kudzu starch and eggs.
4. Make the batter of about the thickness of fritters.
5. Lay these in a pan that has been oiled, and bake in a slow oven for two hours.
6. Make a gravy by chopping the giblets very fine and putting them in a pot with a cupful of water, a cupful of syou sauce, salt and pepper, and a teaspoonful of sugar.
7. Boil slowly while the duck is baking.
8. Place the duck, which should be well browned, on a platter, then pour over it the gravy and garnish with orange or lemon peel, grated.

title: Mushi Kiji (Roast Pheasant)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- One good pheasant
- one pound of small Japanese almonds
- one and one half cupfuls of rice paste
- three cupfuls of mirin sauce
- one half cupful of cooking sauce
- one tablespoonful of sugar
- salt, pepper, and mixed spices
- oil

## Directions

1. Wash and singe the bird, then rub well with salt.
2. Place in deep dish and cover with three cupfuls of mirin sauce; more, if that quantity does not cover the bird.
3. Lay away in a cool place over night, then drain off all liquid.
4. Have ready the almonds, blanched and chopped very fine.
5. Add them to the rice paste with the sugar, a little salt, quarter of a cup of cooking sauce, and a pinch of mixed spices.
6. Stir all together, and stuff the pheasant with this mixture.
7. Sew up, and truss.
8. Have ready boiling hot olive oil or goma-seed oil, pour it over the pheasant, and place in a hot oven.
9. Roast until tender (about one hour), adding little by little the rest of the cooking sauce, and basting frequently.
10. Cook for a half-hour longer, then place on hot platter and garnish with flower petals and water cress.

title: Mushi Kujira (Boiled Whale or Bass)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- Two pounds of fish
- one half teacupful of syou
- orange and lemon skin
- two long, large radishes
- two tablespoonfuls of vinegar
- salt
- dash of cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Take off all bones and slice the fish daintily in long slices, and then in half-inch dice.
2. Sprinkle with salt, and leave for about fifteen minutes.
3. Cut radishes in long, even, delicate strips.
4. Boil for a few minutes, strain, then add half a cupful of syou sauce and two tablespoonfuls of a fine vinegar.
5. When it boils, drop in the fish slices.
6. Boil up, then push to back of range, and, covered tight, let it simmer for half an hour.
7. Grate the peels of half a lemon and half an orange, and sprinkle over the fish, after having removed it to a hot platter.
8. Serve with boiled rice.

title: Mushi Tamana and Buta (Cabbage and Pork)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- One good-sized hard cabbage
- one cupful of syou sauce
- one tablespoonful of sugar
- one pound of salt pork
- one onion
- salt
- pepper
- goma seeds

## Directions

1. First boil the salt pork for one hour, then chop it in small pieces
2. Select a good hard cabbage without blemishes and wash it thoroughly in cold water
3. Soak it in water with a handful of salt to kill all possible vermin
4. Remove from salted water, and rinse in fresh water, removing all salt
5. Carefully cut out the heart and center leaves, making a good-sized pocket, and fill this space with the chopped pork and one onion chopped very fine, salt, pepper, and a teaspoonful of goma seeds
6. Press the cabbage leaves back into place, and tie tight in cheesecloth
7. Have ready a deep pot of boiling salted water, and in this place the cabbage in the bag
8. Cover, and boil slowly for two hours
9. Remove to a platter, and pour over it the syou sauce, which has first been boiled
10. Serve with rice

title: Mushroom Omelette
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoonful of lard
- one quarter of an onion
- one half pound of fresh mushrooms
- one tablespoonful of syou
- four eggs
- one quarter teaspoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Into a tablespoonful of hot, sweet lard fry quarter of an onion a light brown.
2. Cut half a pound of fresh mushrooms very fine, and cook for five minutes.
3. Beat four eggs with one tablespoonful of syou, and turn into pan.
4. Cook for five minutes without touching, but keep from burning by gently shaking the pan.
5. Fold over, or slip from pan into dish which is kept warm over a dish of hot water.

title: Nasubi Yaki (Fried Eggplant)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- eggplant
- ice water
- salt
- beaten eggs
- goma-seed oil
- cooking sauce
- goma seeds
- rice

## Directions

1. Peel the eggplant and cut it in slices.
2. Cover with ice water and a handful of salt.
3. Put on a heavy weight, iron or stone, to press out the juice, and leave for about half an hour.
4. Strain off all water, which will be a dark brown, and rinse with fresh cold water, wiping dry each slice.
5. Dip each piece in beaten eggs, and fry in goma-seed oil; drain off all oil.
6. Place on a platter, covering well with one cupful of cooking sauce, boiled.
7. Sprinkle with goma seeds, and serve with rice.

title: Nasubi Yaki (Fried Eggplant)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- eggplant
- ice water
- salt
- beaten eggs
- goma-seed oil
- one cupful of cooking sauce
- goma seeds
- rice

## Directions

1. Peel the eggplant and cut it in slices
2. Cover with ice water and a handful of salt
3. Put on a heavy weight, iron or stone, to press out the juice, and leave for about half an hour
4. Strain off all water, which will be a dark brown, and rinse with fresh cold water, wiping dry each slice
5. Dip each piece in beaten eggs, and fry in goma-seed oil
6. Drain off all oil
7. Place on a platter, covering well with one cupful of cooking sauce, boiled
8. Sprinkle with goma seeds, and serve with rice

title: Onigara Yaki (Broiled Lobster)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- One lobster
- syou sauce
- salt and pepper
- powdered sanshi spice

## Directions

1. Wash the lobster carefully in cold water.
2. Place in boiling, salted water, and boil very slowly for one hour.
3. Remove from the saucepan, and immediately throw into cold water.
4. Cut in two pieces lengthwise, and dip each half in syou sauce.
5. Place upon gridiron, and toast well both back and front, taking minute care not to blacken skin and fins.
6. Once again cover with syou sauce, sprinkle with sanshi spice and pepper, and serve.

title: Owa Okashi
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet rice
- goma seeds
- barley sugar

## Directions

1. Roast sweet rice over an open fire, same as popcorn.
2. Mix with goma seeds, and hold together in pressed bars with barley sugar.

title: Pepper Steak
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- One and one half pounds of beef
- two tablespoonfuls of beef suet
- three tablespoonfuls of syou
- six green peppers
- one onion

## Directions

1. Take the upper cut of the round of beef and cut into strips about two inches long.
2. Fry in the beef suet for about four minutes, then add one onion, cut fine.
3. Have the green peppers washed and the seeds removed, and cut in small pieces.
4. Turn in with the beef, and add three tablespoonfuls of syou and some salt.
5. Cover tight, and let simmer for ten minutes.

title: Pineapple Fish
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of fish
- one tablespoonful of syou
- one can of preserved pineapple
- salt
- cornstarch or Quong Sang Chong

## Directions

1. Clean a haddock or codfish of about two pounds and take out the bones
2. Rub well with salt, and set in a fish pan
3. Cover well with boiling water, and let it simmer gently for twenty minutes
4. Drain off all the water, and add one tablespoonful of syou and one can of preserved pineapple
5. Let this simmer slowly for fifteen minutes longer
6. Thicken with cornstarch or Quong Sang Chong and serve with rice

title: Pork with Green Peppers
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- One and one half pounds of pork
- two tablespoonfuls of syou
- six green peppers
- one onion
- one teaspoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Cut the pork in strips about two inches long and half an inch thick.
2. Wash the peppers, remove the seeds, and cut into small pieces; then add to the meat with two tablespoonfuls of syou, one onion chopped fine, and a tablespoonful of salt.
3. Cover, and cook for ten minutes.
4. Serve with rice.

title: Pumpkin and Yellow Seeds
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- large seeds
- salt

## Directions

1. Remove large seeds and spread them on paper.
2. Sprinkle with salt, and bake them until dry.

title: Rice and Nut Candies
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled rice
- chopped nuts
- boiling sirup

## Directions

1. These are made chiefly of boiled rice pressed into balls with chopped nuts, tossed into boiling sirup, and then allowed to cool and harden.

title: Ringo-Kan (Apple Jelly, Japanese Style)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- one half pound seaweed jelly
- three quarters pound sugar
- two big tart apples
- two tablespoonfuls kudzu starch
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash half a pound of seaweed jelly and soak it in water for about three hours, then squeeze it dry and break in small pieces into a pan with one and a half quarts of water.
2. Boil over medium heat.
3. Add three quarters of a pound of sugar, stirring the while, and a pinch of salt.
4. Prepare two big, tart apples by peeling and removing the cores, then rub them on a nutmeg grater until they are reduced to powder.
5. Transfer jelly to another saucepan, strain it, and mix with powdered apple.

title: Roast Chicken (Japanese Style)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- young, tender chicken
- rice
- chicken gravy
- chopped onion
- mixed spices
- salt
- pepper
- cayenne
- roasted sweet potatoes

## Directions

1. Clean and singe the chicken.
2. Put the feet to boil until the scabs scale off, then place with the giblets to boil slowly.
3. Make a stuffing of rice boiled dry in chicken gravy, add chopped onion and a pinch of mixed spices, salt, pepper, and a dash of cayenne.
4. Put this in body of chicken and sew up.
5. Roast for an hour, or longer, according to age of chicken.
6. Make a gravy from the stock of the giblets.
7. Chop the giblets up fine and add to the gravy.
8. Serve with roasted sweet potatoes.

title: Roast Squab
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- One plump squab
- Two chicken livers
- One dozen fresh mushrooms
- One tablespoonful of Chinese almonds
- One tablespoonful of chicken fat
- Two tablespoonfuls of syou
- One teaspoonful of salt
- One quarter small onion
- One teaspoonful of cornstarch

## Directions

1. Clean and singe a plump squab.
2. Rub inside and out with salt.
3. Stuff it with the following: A dozen mushrooms, peeled and cut small, a tablespoonful of almonds which have been blanched and chopped fine, and a little minced onion.
4. Mix together, and fill the squab.
5. Now sew or skew tight, and melt the tablespoonful of chicken fat and pour it over the squab.
6. Place in a hot oven and roast for half an hour, basting and turning frequently.
7. Make the gravy, meanwhile, by chopping fine two chicken livers and frying them in a teaspoonful of chicken fat.
8. When very brown, stir in a teaspoonful of cornstarch until brown.
9. Now add two tablespoonfuls of syou, and serve with the squab.

title: Sakura (Cherry Custard)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- six fresh eggs
- one cupful of milk
- sugar
- three tablespoonfuls of cherry sirup
- ripe, plump red cherries

## Directions

1. Beat up lightly and frothily six fresh eggs
2. Add one cupful of milk, sugar, and three tablespoonfuls of cherry sirup
3. Throw a handful of ripe, plump red cherries into the baking pan, and over this pour the above mixture
4. Bake for about half an hour in a medium oven
5. Remove, and at intervals over top of dish set more cherries

title: San Char Go
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- red Chinese dates
- powdered sugar
- gelatine

## Directions

1. The fruit is first mashed to a pulp, then mixed with powdered sugar and gelatine, beaten into a paste, rolled in sheets as thin as paper, and spread in the sun to dry.
2. After it has dried crisp, it is cut into squares of six by four inches, wrapped in waxed paper, and packed in bundles.

title: Satsuma Soup
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- one small chicken
- one medium-sized carrot
- one pint of satorius (elephant-ear plant)
- two white radishes
- one quarter pound of dried mushrooms
- one quarter pound of aburage (oil-fried Tofu)
- one quarter pound of miso paste (soy bean and rice cheese)
- one half teaspoonful of mixed red-pepper spices
- salt
- sugar

## Directions

1. To prepare the chicken, separate the meat from the bones; then put bones in cold water and boil slowly for about two hours.
2. Strain, and add the well-chopped vegetables. (The mushrooms should have been washed, and soaked in lukewarm water for ten minutes previously.)
3. Boil for one more hour, then add the chicken meat, cut in very small pieces, salt, spices, and half a teaspoonful of sugar.
4. Cook all slowly for one hour.

title: Satsuma Tamana (Cabbage, Satsuma Style)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- hard crisp cabbage
- goma-seed or olive oil
- stoned prunes
- lemon juice
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash and shred fine a hard, crisp cabbage.
2. In deep pan put goma-seed or olive oil.
3. Add the cabbage, with half a dozen stoned prunes and the juice of a lemon, pepper, and salt.
4. Let this slowly stew in its own juices for an hour or so.

title: Scrambled Eggs (Japanese Style)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- green pepper
- cold boiled rice
- mushrooms
- onion

## Directions

1. Break into frying pan half a dozen eggs, stirring lightly with knife
2. Add quickly, before egg begins to cook, a tablespoonful of chopped green pepper, the juice of an onion, half a cupful of chopped mushrooms, and half a cupful of boiled rice
3. Toss all quickly together
4. Cook over a very slow fire, and serve hot

title: Seaweed Soup
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- two yolks of hard-boiled eggs
- one can of seaweed
- three chicken giblets
- two tablespoonfuls of syou
- one and one half teaspoonfuls of Quong Sang Chong (water chestnut flour)
- salt to taste
- spiced vinegar

## Directions

1. Boil one can of seaweed until it is like thin jelly
2. Have ready three chicken giblets, chopped very fine, having first boiled them one hour in a quart of water
3. Add the seaweed, and boil all together for half an hour
4. Strain, then crumble in the yolks of two hard-boiled eggs
5. Stir in two tablespoonfuls of syou, and salt to taste
6. Rub smooth one and a half teaspoonfuls of Quong Sang Chong in a little cold water, then add to the soup and stir until it thickens slightly
7. Serve with a small piece of seaweed on top that has been soaking in spiced vinegar

title: Shika Shiro (Pot-roasted Venison)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- Three pounds of venison steak
- one cupful of syou sauce
- one cupful of vinegar
- two tablespoonfuls of sugar
- one quarter pound of fresh pork (fat)
- one quarter pound of miso paste
- one onion
- salt
- mixed spices
- lemon
- parsley

## Directions

1. Place the meat in an earthenware bowl and cover with one cup of vinegar, two tablespoonfuls of sugar and the same amount of salt, one tablespoonful of mixed spices, and one onion, sliced.
2. Cover, and put away to pickle for two or three days in a cool place.
3. When ready to cook, remove meat from the juices, cut up the pork in small strips, and fry a light brown in deep iron pan.
4. Add the venison meat, and fry well on both sides until brown, taking care not to burn it.
5. Add the syou sauce, cover tight, and let simmer for one hour, or two, if necessary.
6. Mix the miso paste with a little cold water, and stir it until it thickens.
7. Remove the platter, and garnish with slices of lemon sprinkled with chopped parsley.

title: Shiro Uri (Stewed Squash)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- squash or vegetable marrows
- salt
- syou sauce
- chopped meat or fish
- miso
- pepper
- spices
- lemon

## Directions

1. Peel and boil squash in salted water for twenty minutes
2. Remove and allow to simmer in boiling syou sauce for five minutes longer
3. To stuff one, take cold meat, chop it very fine, then mix with it two tablespoonfuls of miso and salt, pepper, and spices, and two tablespoonfuls of syou, and fill the center
4. Fish can be used instead of meat; but if fish is used, add the juice of a lemon

title: Shrimp Omelette
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- one half cup of shrimp meat
- five eggs
- one cupful of mushrooms
- two tablespoonfuls of sweet lard
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Put the sweet lard in the pan, and when hot throw in the mushrooms, which have been first washed and cut small
2. Cook for five minutes, then add the shrimp meat, which has previously been cooked and flaked
3. Cook for two minutes more
4. Beat five eggs and throw in the pan, and cook for five minutes, taking care not to burn, but do not stir or shake the pan

title: Shu Bok Ap (Fried Squab)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- Two squabs
- Olive or peanut oil
- Two chicken livers
- One small onion
- Two tablespoonfuls of syou
- One tablespoonful of Chinese almonds
- One quarter pound of white mushrooms
- One teaspoonful of salt
- Yolks of two eggs, hard-boiled

## Directions

1. Cut the squabs in pieces, not too small, Let the legs be separated from the body, also the wings, and cut the rest in four pieces.
2. Singe off all hair and feathers.
3. Wash in cold water, and wipe dry.
4. Dip each piece of squab in dry flour, and toss in boiling olive or peanut oil.
5. Cook until it is crisp.
6. Drain off all fat, while preparing the following: Put one tablespoonful of olive oil in the pan, and when it is hot, place the chicken livers (chopped very fine) in the fat, add a small minced onion, two tablespoonfuls of syou, a tablespoonful of Chinese small almonds, blanched and chopped, and a quarter of a pound of white mushrooms, cut small.
7. Fry for ten minutes, then pour into hot dish and place the squab on top, garnished with crumbled hard-boiled yolks of eggs.

title: Soup Stock
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- beef bones
- veal bones
- chicken bones
- clean pieces of meat
- feet of chickens
- giblets
- cold water
- salt

## Directions

1. Save all beef, veal, and chicken bones and clean pieces of meat; also the feet of chickens and the giblets
2. Wash the chicken feet and skin them, by first scalding in boiling water
3. Cover all the above with cold water and let it boil from five to eight hours slowly, with salt to taste
4. Drain liquor from bones, and set away to cool
5. When cold it will be a thick jelly, and is used as the foundation for various soups and gravies

title: Steamed Duck
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- Three and one half pound duck
- One quarter pound of pork
- One quarter pound of mushrooms
- One quarter pound of water chestnuts
- One quarter cup of barley
- One and one half tablespoonfuls of syou
- One teaspoonful of salt

## Directions

1. After the duck has been well cleaned, inside and out, cut down the breast, dry well, and rub it thoroughly with salt.
2. Lay in a bowl, and then prepare the following filling: Take one quarter pound of pork, chopped fine; one quarter pound of mushrooms, cut small, and one quarter pound of water chestnuts, sliced thin.
3. Boil one quarter pound of barley for ten minutes, adding half a tablespoonful of syou and a teaspoonful of salt.
4. Mix together all of the above ingredients, fill the duck, and sew or skew it up.
5. Steam for three hours, and serve with rice.

title: Stuffed Bananas
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- long plump bananas
- shredded coconut
- cream
- sugar
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Bake long, plump bananas for ten minutes in medium oven.
2. Remove and split skin down one side.
3. Remove the inside, and whip it up with shredded coconut, a little cream and sugar, and a little lemon juice.
4. Return to shell, and bake for another ten minutes.

title: Sumomo Sui and Amai (Plums, Sour and Sweet)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- sugar
- vinegar
- mixed spices
- plums
- mirin sauce

## Directions

1. Have a sirup made of half a cupful of water, two cupfuls of sugar, a quarter cupful of vinegar, and half a teaspoonful of mixed spices, ground.
2. When it is boiling drop in the plums, and let boil slowly for twenty minutes.
3. Add half a cupful of mirin sauce, or any other desired spirits, and let all come to a boil once.
4. Remove, and serve.

title: Sweet and Pungent Chicken
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- Three and one half pound chicken
- one dessert-spoonful of salt
- two tablespoonfuls of sugar
- two cupfuls of vinegar
- one half onion
- one tablespoonful of sweet lard
- two tablespoonfuls of syou

## Directions

1. Take a young chicken of about three and one half pounds.
2. Clean and singe it, and remove all the bones.
3. Lay in a stone dish and over it pour (enough to cover well) a mixture of two cupfuls of boiling vinegar, a dessert-spoonful of salt, and two tablespoonfuls of sugar.
4. Cover, and put away in a cool place for twenty-four hours.
5. Prepare a brown gravy by chopping half an onion and frying it a light brown in sweet lard or chicken fat.
6. Cut the chicken into quarters, and put in a pan with two tablespoonfuls of syou.
7. Simmer for half an hour very gently.
8. Serve with rice.

title: Sweet Potato Candy
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet potatoes
- sirup

## Directions

1. Boil sweet potatoes, mash and roll them into little balls, then toss into boiling sirup.
2. Let them brown.
3. Take out, and set to cool and become brittle.

title: Tamago Bolan (Peony Eggs)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- five eggs
- small pink snapper (fish)
- miso
- pepper
- salt
- udo shoots
- lettuce head

## Directions

1. Boil five eggs hard
2. Place in cold water
3. Remove shells carefully, so as not to blemish whites
4. Carefully cut off top with thread, one end between teeth, the other between fingers, drawing thread through egg
5. Remove the yolks
6. Boil a small pink snapper (fish) in hot water for ten minutes, or steam for thirty
7. Remove all bones and fins, and chop together until fine
8. Mix with finely mashed miso, pepper, and salt
9. Chop yolks daintily and fluffily, and mix with fish meat
10. Fill the whites with this mixture
11. Place the filled whites in center of a lettuce head and arrange fine strips of udo shoots round it
12. Carefully adjust and separate all the leaves of the lettuce head, wash, and then put back into shape again
13. Hold together with toothpicks

title: Tamago Tofu
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- cooking juice
- syou sauce
- horseradish

## Directions

1. Break any required number of eggs and beat with chopsticks
2. Mix with twice the amount of cooking juice and one third of syou sauce
3. Pour the mixture into a thin china bowl or a tin mold, and steam over boiling water for thirty or forty minutes
4. Transfer a soupladleful to each individual bowl, and over each pour a small quantity of cooking juice and a few drops of syou sauce
5. Sprinkle with ground horseradish, and serve very hot

title: Tea
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- tea leaves
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Rinse the pot with boiling water before putting in the tea
2. The water should just come to a boil—no more
3. Pour on the tea leaves
4. Let it stand for two or three minutes, but on no account must it be allowed to boil
5. Strain tea from leaves, and serve pure, if Chinese tea is used

title: Tembani of Mackerel (Fish Delicacy)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- One good-sized mackerel
- one fresh radish
- one cupful of syou sauce
- vinegar
- orange or lemon peel
- one half cupful of cooking juice
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean the fish, and remove all bones.
2. Cut into half-inch dice, sprinkle with salt, and let stand for about half an hour.
3. Wash the radish and cut into thin pieces, then throw it into the salted, boiling water.
4. Boil for about five minutes, then strain off all water and add half a cupful of syou sauce and the same amount of cooking juice.
5. Let all boil up, then add the fish and let it simmer for about twenty-five minutes.
6. Remove from the fire, and before serving add a tablespoonful of vinegar.
7. Place on a hot platter, and sprinkle with grated lemon or orange peel.
8. Serve with boiled rice.

title: Ten Sune Gune (Sweet and Sour Fish)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of sea bass
- one and one half cupfuls of water
- one tablespoonful of Quong Sang Chong (water-chestnut flour)
- two and one half tablespoonfuls of vinegar
- one tablespoonful of salt
- one and one half tablespoonfuls of sugar

## Directions

1. Clean a sea bass of about two pounds and take out all the insides, taking care to keep the fish whole
2. Put it into a medium deep dish large enough to fit the fish
3. Pour over it water almost boiling, cover fish well over, put a lid on dish, and leave on range for one hour
4. Do not boil, but keep it hot
5. Rub smooth one tablespoonful of Quong Sang Chong
6. To a cup and a half of water add one tablespoonful of salt, one and one half tablespoonfuls of sugar, and two and one half tablespoonfuls of vinegar
7. Mix well and boil until it thickens, stirring constantly to prevent burning
8. Dish up the fish, place on another hot, dry dish, then pour over it the sauce, and serve with rice

title: To Boil Rice
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- iron pot
- rice
- one cupful of rice
- one and one quarter cupfuls of cold water
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash the rice thoroughly in many waters, until the water is as clear as for drinking
2. To one cupful of rice add one and one quarter cupfuls of cold water
3. Cover tight, and boil slowly half an hour—no more
4. On no account stir
5. Set on back of range, covered with a clean cloth or napkin, until ready to serve
6. Rice should be served in individual bowls, replenished as soon as empty throughout the meal
7. Salt is not added until it is served

title: Tori Shiru (Chicken Soup)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- two pound chicken
- one half pound of egg noodles
- one half cupful syou sauce
- one quarter pound of white mushrooms
- one half dozen water chestnuts
- salt
- pepper
- sugar
- flour
- eggs

## Directions

1. Thoroughly clean and singe the chicken, and scald feet to peel off scabs or skin.
2. Cut giblets in small pieces and the chicken in about six pieces, and cover with three quarts of cold water.
3. Boil all for about three hours, according to the age of the chicken.
4. While it is boiling wash and cut up the vegetables.
5. Now make the egg noodles of flour and eggs and roll as thin as paper on a floured board, then cut in shapes of flowers (chrysanthemums, the Japanese prefer), and leave spread out on the board to dry.
6. Drain all soup from the bones and bring to a boil.
7. Add vegetables, salt, pepper, and one teaspoonful of sugar; also the syou sauce.
8. Boil all for half an hour, then throw in the noodles and cook for ten minutes.
9. Serve each bowl of soup with one large noodle flower on top.

title: Tori-Kan (Chicken Jelly)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds chicken flesh
- one half pound seaweed jelly or gelatine
- one tablespoonful sugar
- salt
- pepper
- one quarter pound dried mushrooms
- one cup mirin sauce
- water

## Directions

1. Cook in boiling water a young, tender chicken until it is tender.
2. Take from the liquid, remove all the bone and cut in small pieces.
3. Pick off stalks from mushrooms and soak in lukewarm water for twenty minutes.
4. Cut in small pieces and boil for twenty-five minutes in about two cupfuls of water.
5. Add the mirin sauce and boil again about five minutes.
6. Wash the dry seaweed jelly and soak for about two hours, then take from water and squeeze dry.
7. Break into small pieces and boil in one quart of water until it dissolves.
8. Strain into another saucepan, with a pinch of salt and a tablespoonful of sugar.
9. To this add the mushrooms and mirin sauce, and mix all together, with the chicken meat, salt, pepper, spices, etc.
10. Have ready a lacquered box or smooth wooden box or mold, and pour in the mixture.
11. Let it set, and, when serving, cut it in slices.

title: Unohana Yaki (Flower Custard)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- Two ounces of Kanton (Japanese gelatine)
- six eggs
- three tablespoonfuls of cherry sirup
- one ounce of goma seeds

## Directions

1. To make the pink custards, whip the whites of five eggs into the dissolved two ounces of gelatine.
2. Add, while stirring over a slow fire, three tablespoonfuls of cherry sirup.
3. Let it come to a boil, then pour quickly into flower-shaped molds, and let it set.
4. Yellow custards can be made by using the yolks instead of the whites of eggs, and half a pint of coconut milk.
5. When the custards are solid, turn them from the molds on to large leaves on a plate, or decorate the flowered custard with leaves suitable to the flower represented.
6. If the flower has a dark center, use goma seeds, and if variegated colors are liked, pour half the yellow and half the cherry custards into the molds.
7. The pouring should be done very quickly and lightly, and the molds, of course, should be of flower designs.

title: Usagi Amai-Sui (Hare, Sweet and Sour)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- One good-sized rabbit or hare
- one cupful of vinegar
- one cupful of syou sauce
- one tablespoonful of salt
- three tablespoonfuls of sugar
- one half cupful of mirin sauce
- one tablespoonful of mixed spices
- one half pound of fat fresh pork
- one dozen red plums
- one small lime or lemon

## Directions

1. Take a small hare or large rabbit that has been hanging for at least two days, and after it has been skinned, and the insides removed, wash well in cold water.
2. Cut in quarters and put in a deep dish, covering it well with the vinegar, sugar, salt, and so on, mixed with water.
3. Add the spices also, and a quarter of a cupful of mirin sauce.
4. Cover up well, and let it pickle in a cool place for two or three days.
5. Remove it from the liquid and cut in small pieces.
6. Have ready the fat pork cut in small pieces, and fry.
7. Toss in the hare, and brown.
8. Have ready a covered China cook dish or casserole, and place in it the hare and pork, adding the syou sauce, a pinch of salt, pinch of the spices, and the quarter cupful of mirin sauce.
9. Cover up tightly, and cook in a slow oven for one hour.
10. Then prepare a dozen large red plums, removing the stones, and add them.
11. Dissolve one teaspoonful of Kanton and mix with the gravy, taking care not to break the plums.
12. Cook for another five minutes, then place on a hot platter, decorated with slices of lemon or limes, with a plum placed on top of each slice.
13. This is good served either with white-bean cakes or rice.

title: Usu Tamago Yaki (Fried Eggs)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- One half dozen eggs
- one cupful of cooking juice
- two tablespoonfuls of syou sauce

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with chopsticks, adding the cooking sauce and syou
2. Mix all well
3. Wipe a frying pan with paper soaked with goma-seed oil
4. As soon as pan is well heated, pour in the above mixture and fry over a slow fire

title: Water Chestnut Salad
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- water chestnuts
- two tablespoonfuls of olive oil
- one onion
- one tablespoonful of fine vinegar
- two teaspoonfuls of syou
- one teaspoonful of salt
- yolks of two hard-boiled eggs
- one half teaspoonful of sugar
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Peel and wash these little nuts, which are about the size of an ordinary chestnut.
2. Slice very thin.
3. Make a dressing of the following: To two tablespoonfuls of olive oil add the juice of one onion, one tablespoonful of fine vinegar, two teaspoonfuls of syou, one teaspoonful of salt, the yolks of two hard-boiled eggs crushed smooth, and half a teaspoonful of sugar.
4. Mix all until smooth, then pour it over the water chestnuts.
5. Sprinkle with chopped parsley.

title: Yaki Udzura (Broiled Quail or Pigeons)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- Quail or pigeons
- mushrooms
- aburage (bean cheese paste) Tofu
- olive oil
- parsley
- salt
- dry flour

## Directions

1. Prepare the quail or pigeons the day before using by washing and singeing and rubbing with salt, then put them away in cool place.
2. Next day take half a pound of aburage, and fry the Tofu cakes in oil.
3. Set in warm place to drain off the oil while toasting the birds.
4. Dip the birds in olive oil, then in dry flour, and toast over a red-hot fire, turning quickly until they are a crisp brown.
5. Place each bird, or half of a bird, if it is a pigeon, on the fried Tofu, and have some white mushrooms which have been fried in oil, placed around the birds.
6. Sprinkle with chopped parsley, and serve very hot.

title: Yaki Zakana (Fried Fish)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- small fish
- salt
- peanut or goma-seed oil, or olive oil
- oil-fried Tofu
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Wash the fish and scale it if necessary, removing all fins.
2. Rub well with salt.
3. Have ready a deep iron pot and use peanut or goma-seed oil, or, if preferred, olive oil.
4. Do not have too much oil in pan, but only enough to prevent burning.
5. Lay the fish in the pan, but do not crowd one on top of another; fry a light brown, being careful in turning not to break the fish.
6. Have ready oil-fried Tofu, and place each fish on a cake of about the length of the fish.
7. Sprinkle with grated lemon peel, and serve with rice, very hot.

title: Yat Ko Main
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of soup stock
- one quarter pound of noodles
- one and one half teaspoonfuls of salt
- one quarter pound of minced ham
- one tablespoonful of syou
- one small onion
- one stalk of celery
- pepper

## Directions

1. Boil the noodles until tender—about ten minutes
2. Remove from the hot water, and put in cold water to soak
3. To one quart of good soup stock add one onion and one stalk of celery, chopped fine, pepper and salt, and one tablespoonful of syou
4. Boil ten minutes
5. Remove noodles from the cold water, add them to the soup, and boil for five minutes more
6. Shred the ham
7. Just before serving sprinkle it on top

title: Yea Foo Main
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of soup stock
- one quarter pound of mushrooms
- three stalks of celery
- one eighth clove of garlic
- six water chestnuts
- one quarter pound of vermicelli
- one and one quarter teaspoonfuls of salt
- one teaspoonful of syou
- pepper
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Have the soup stock boiling hot
2. Wash thoroughly in cold water all the vegetables, and cut in small pieces
3. Add to the soup one tablespoonful of syou, and the salt and pepper
4. Let all boil twenty minutes
5. Add the vermicelli and boil fifteen minutes more
6. Serve with hard-boiled eggs cut in quarters

title: Yeu (Fish Cakes)
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- one cupful of fish meat
- two cupfuls of rice flour
- two tablespoonfuls of peanut oil
- one and one half teaspoonfuls of salt
- one quarter pound of almonds
- cold water

## Directions

1. Take any dried fish, and remove all bones and skin
2. Pound the fish to a powder, and mix one cup of the fish with a quarter pound of blanched almonds, also pounded
3. Add two cupfuls of dried and powdered rice flour, and half a teaspoonful of salt
4. Beat the whites of two eggs with a little cold water, enough to make a thin paste
5. Mix well all together
6. Roll very thin, cut into cakes, and bake in a hot oven in hot peanut oil for twenty minutes
7. Remove the cakes from the oil, and drain on paper in a slow oven until cakes are dry and crisp

title: Yohan Candy
author: Sara Bosse & Onoto Watanna
body:
## Ingredients

- adzuki (purple beans)
- sugar
- seaweed gelatine

## Directions

1. Boil adzuki (purple beans) to a paste, and mix with sugar and seaweed gelatine.
2. Allow it to harden.
